content: Hepatitis B virus (HBV) is a partially double-stranded DNA hepadnavirus causing acute and chronic hepatitis. Transmission: percutaneous, sexual, perinatal (high chronicity risk if infected at birth). Natural history: incubation 60-150 days; acute infection resolves in 95% of immunocompetent adults (HBsAg clearance, anti-HBs development); 5% develop chronic infection (HBsAg >6 months). Chronic phases: immune tolerant (HBeAg+, high DNA, normal ALT), immune active (HBeAg+/anti-HBe+, elevated ALT, liver inflammation), inactive carrier (HBeAg-, anti-HBe+, low DNA, normal ALT), HBeAg-negative chronic hepatitis (mutant virus, fluctuating DNA/ALT). Complications: cirrhosis (15-40%), hepatocellular carcinoma (annual risk 2-8% in cirrhosis). Vaccination highly effective (95% seroprotection); universal infant vaccination implemented globally.

Acute HBV: supportive care; antivirals only for severe/fulminant cases. Chronic HBV treatment indications: HBeAg+ with ALT >2x ULN and DNA >20,000 IU/mL; HBeAg- with ALT >2x ULN and DNA >2,000 IU/mL; any cirrhosis with detectable DNA; extrahepatic manifestations; immunosuppression prophylaxis. First-line agents: tenofovir alafenamide (TAF) 25mg daily, tenofovir disoproxil fumarate (TDF) 300mg daily, or entecavir 0.5mg daily (1mg if lamivudine-resistant). Pegylated interferon-alpha finite course (48 weeks) for selected patients. Treatment goals: HBV DNA suppression, ALT normalization, HBeAg seroconversion, ideally HBsAg loss ('functional cure'). Duration often indefinite; may stop in select HBeAg+ after seroconversion + consolidation. Monitor for resistance, renal/bone toxicity (TDF), hepatocellular carcinoma surveillance every 6 months.

HBV serologic markers: HBsAg (current infection), anti-HBs (immunity), anti-HBc IgM (acute infection), anti-HBc total/IgG (past/current infection), HBeAg (active replication), anti-HBe (reduced replication), HBV DNA (viral load quantification). Acute HBV: HBsAg+, anti-HBc IgM+, HBeAg+/variable. Resolved infection: HBsAg-, anti-HBs+, anti-HBc IgG+. Vaccinated: anti-HBs+ only. Chronic HBV: HBsAg+ >6 months, anti-HBc IgG+, anti-HBc IgM-. 'Window period': HBsAg cleared, anti-HBs not yet detectable, only anti-HBc IgM positive. HBV DNA quantification guides treatment decisions and monitors response. Genotyping not routinely needed. Liver biopsy or non-invasive fibrosis assessment (FibroScan, FIB-4) stages disease. Screening recommended for high-risk populations and all pregnant women.
